title: Linting for Correctness, Safety, and Maintainability · Policy Clinic: Recommended Baseline
summary: Configure a high-signal linter beyond default style warnings. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates recommended baseline increment in a risk-based lint policy and fixtures. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Start with maintained recommended rules and promote material findings to errors.
Introduce or replace formatting with isolated reviewable migration commits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Predict Recommended Baseline
steps.instruction:

steps.title: Build Recommended Baseline evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-recommended-baseline-qrqa7s: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Recommended Baseline into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-recommended-baseline-qrqa7s:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the Recommended Baseline model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Recommended Baseline applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Start with maintained recommended rules and promote material findings to errors.
